_meta.purpose: Single editable source of truth for the day orchestrator. Cadence, priority, and freshness are deliberately KNOBS — tuned by hand during the ~1-week parallel dry-run (Megan 2026-06-23).
_meta.scope: ALL non-upload reports run on the scheduler (Megan 2026-06-23). Frontier + Financial moved to auto email-ingest 2026-07-01. First/Last Sale RETIRED 2026-07-06.
_meta.weekday_convention: Python weekday(): Mon=0, Tue=1, Wed=2, Thu=3, Fri=4, Sat=5, Sun=6 (matches dashboard.py).
_meta.times: All times are CST (the mini is Central). HH:MM 24h.
_meta.priority_tiers: P1 critical (guaranteed done daily, runs first) > P2 standard > P3 best-effort (runs last).
_meta.freshness_target: A DEADLINE distinct from cadence. e.g. 'monday_am' = must be current by Monday morning even if deprioritized midweek; the orchestrator escalates effective priority as the deadline nears.
_meta.source_type: tableau = readiness gate + circle-back applies | appstream = immediately ready (no probe) | api = ready | upload = MANUAL, never auto-run.
_meta.run_order_flow: Run order optimizes TOTAL runtime (registry.flow_rank): wave 1 = every NON-tableau report (appstream / api / email / upload) so their early-ready data fills first; wave 2 = all source_type 'tableau' reports LAST, giving Tableau the most time to finish publishing before we read it; daily_rep_breakdown is pinned dead last (heaviest, flakiest). New reports auto-map by source_type — set it correctly and the report lands in the right wave automatically; no per-report order field. Source-type-based, never weekday-based, so a weekend-scheduled AppStream report still runs in wave 1 and is never blocked.
_meta.probe_not_configured: A tableau source with probe.type 'not_configured' resolves READY in --dry-run (so the full loop exercises) and NOT-READY in live (forces wiring the real view URL before cutover).
_meta.cutover_retire_jobs: These now run from the orchestrator — RETIRE their standalone LaunchAgents at cutover so reports don't double-run/double-post: com.alphalete.appstream-morning (daily_focus + Mon recruiter retention), com.alphalete.weather-6am, com.alphalete.brand-audit-noon, com.alphalete.recruiting-report (weekly ATT). Until cutover (dry-run week) they coexist safely — the orchestrator writes nothing.

settings._recipients_note: Send only to alphaletereporting@ — direct Gmail-to-Gmail sends to personal inboxes get silently dropped. A filter in that account (from:alphaletereporting to:alphaletereporting) forwards every report email to Meganhidalgo1191@. Add to the FORWARD, not here.

probe._todo: wire view_url + crosstab_sheet + date_col + min_rows before live cutover

tableau:box_daily.label: BOX daily tracker (org sales board — last source to land, ~7-8am)

vantura_payroll._note: Vantura Weekly Payroll PREP (Carlos). on_scheduler:false = the 4am orchestrator NEVER runs it — its real schedule is the Wednesday 11:00am CT launchd job (com.alphalete.vantura-payroll-wed, wrapper deploy/vantura_payroll_wed.sh) on Lucy 2. DRY-RUN GATED: automations.vantura_payroll.run defaults to --dry-run (no board writes, no Slack); the board-write internals (RAW load incl. self-pulled Tableau crosstab, Commission!B1, per-campaign P&L formulas, headless Refresh via the Payroll.gs web app, read-only checks, kickoff DM) are implemented but stay dry-run until verified against a SANDBOX copy of the Vantura Master Sales Board (see automations/vantura_payroll/PAYROLL_RUNBOOK.md). Reuses vantura_churn's CDP Tableau pull + recruiting_report.fill board auth + shared.slack_metrics_post (as Lucy). Automates the deterministic prep only; bonuses/no-pay/rates + verify + print stay with Carlos, and the week auto-locks Thursday ~11am via the board's own Apps Script trigger. This handle exists so `lucy rerun vantura_payroll --dry-run` works.

install_vantura_payroll_agent._off_scheduler_note: on_scheduler:false so it is NOT in the 4am batch. Present only so `lucy rerun install_vantura_payroll_agent` can install/refresh the STANDALONE com.alphalete.vantura-payroll-wed LaunchAgent on Lucy 2, which runs the Vantura payroll PREP (automations.vantura_payroll.run) Wednesday 11:00am machine-local time via deploy/vantura_payroll_wed.sh. The wrapper passes no mode flag, so the module's --dry-run default applies until sandbox sign-off flips it to --live in the wrapper. install_agent is read-only w.r.t. any Sheet/report data; it only reloads the plist. Re-run this after the plist changes.

car_rides._note: Car-Rides Cleanup (Carlos). on_scheduler:false = the 4am orchestrator NEVER runs it — its real schedule is the Mon-Sat launchd job (com.alphalete.car-rides-cleanup, wrapper deploy/car_rides_cleanup.sh) on Lucy 2: 9 morning passes at 8:30/9:00/9:30/10:00/10:30/10:40/10:50/11:00/11:15, with a skip-if-still-running guard in the wrapper. Reconciles each car-ride leader's OwnerVille/TeleMapper territory (index.cfm?p=158) against the Stations tab of the Vantura Master Sales Board, both campaigns (B2B AT&T SBS + B2B-BOX-Energy). Fully unattended by design: board via recruiting_report.fill gspread, OwnerVille via tableau_patchright's exported storage_state (session-holder keeps it warm) — a stale/missing session is FLAGGED, never re-authed (no form / Turnstile). DRY-RUN default: plans + reports to output/car_rides/, changes nothing; --live applies select2 add/remove+Save edits. Never creates or Removes territories (flag-only), never touches Assign Clients / sharing / Slack. This handle exists so `lucy rerun car_rides --dry-run` and `--probe` work.

harvest_proof._off_scheduler_note: SHADOW/INERT harvest-once proof (Stage c of output/harvest-architecture-design.md). on_scheduler:false = NEVER scheduled, structurally excluded from the 4am orchestrator (registry.load_config drops it; run_order never sees it) so it CANNOT affect the morning run. Run only by hand: `lucy rerun harvest_proof` (5-need structural cover) or `lucy rerun harvest_proof --full` (all 19 churn pulls). Runs automations.harvest.proof_mini: harvest the churn cluster once over one login, then diff live-pull-parse vs cache-parse cell-for-cell + a monkeypatch cutover check. Full result -> output/logs/harvest_proof-<date>.log (retrieve with `lucy logtail harvest_proof`); compact verdict lands in `lucy status`. source_type tableau only so the rerun runs chrome_guard first. Nothing production imports automations.harvest.

install_harvest_proof_agent._off_scheduler_note: on_scheduler:false so it is NOT in the 4am batch (the batch behavior is unchanged). Present only so `lucy rerun install_harvest_proof_agent` can install/refresh the STANDALONE com.alphalete.harvest-proof-1pm LaunchAgent on the mini, which runs the SHADOW harvest proof (automations.harvest.proof_mini) daily at 1pm CT — after the noon backstop, so no shared-Chrome-profile collision with the batch. install_agent is read-only w.r.t. any Sheet/report data; it only reloads the plist. Re-run this after the plist changes.

rebuild_lastweek._note: MANUAL-ONLY recovery — registered so it can run on the MINI via lucy (the ownerville session lives there; a laptop scrape knocks out the session every other report shares). Replays the known-good Monday sequence for a target week: label top as last week -> scrape last week (ownerville) -> [Tableau, full run only] -> FREEZE (HARD-GATED: aborts before the wipe if the freeze writes 0 tabs) -> label top as this week -> wipe -> scrape this week. Use when a tab's frozen LAST WEEK block is STRUCTURALLY broken (nested/duplicate 'LAST WEEK' labels, reps stranded far below) — drb_backfill_lastweek only refills activity by rep name and CANNOT fix structure. Preview: `lucy rerun rebuild_lastweek --monday 2026-07-06 --plan`. One tab: `lucy rerun rebuild_lastweek --monday 2026-07-06 --only '"Kiarri McBroom"'` (note the literal quotes — lucy joins argv, shlex re-splits). WARNING: --only SKIPS Tableau (org-wide, unscopable), so the rebuilt frozen block has NO SALES — a frozen block is a static snapshot and can't be sales-patched after. Only use --only on a tab you've confirmed had zero sales that week. Registered 2026-07-07.
rebuild_lastweek.display_name: Daily Rep Breakdown — rebuild frozen LAST WEEK block

drb_backfill_lastweek._note: MANUAL-ONLY recovery. `lucy rerun drb_backfill_lastweek <MONDAY>` re-pulls last week's Tue-Sun activity from ownerville and writes it into the frozen bottom LAST WEEK block (matched by rep name), touching NEITHER this week NOR the totals/Monday. For when last week's daily scrapes failed (e.g. the mini-sleep gaps) and the LAST WEEK block shows 'x' for Tue-Sun. Preview one tab first: `lucy rerun drb_backfill_lastweek 2026-06-29 --only "Rafael Hidalgo"`; then full: `lucy rerun drb_backfill_lastweek 2026-06-29`. Per-owner 5-min cap + 150-min report timeout. Built 2026-07-06.
drb_backfill_lastweek.display_name: Daily Rep Breakdown — backfill LAST WEEK Tue-Sun

install_keep_awake_agent._note: MANUAL-ONLY. `lucy rerun install_keep_awake_agent` installs the com.alphalete.keep-awake LaunchAgent on the mini (user-level, NO root) — runs `caffeinate -s` 24/7 so the mini never sleeps and launchd's 4am orchestrator isn't deferred to ~6am. The remote, no-sudo alternative to the pmset wake schedule (set_wake, which needs root the mini won't give). Diagnosed via morning_diag 2026-07-06.

set_wake._note: MANUAL-ONLY (weekdays []). `lucy rerun set_wake` sets a daily 03:55 pmset wakeorpoweron schedule on the mini so the 4am orchestrator isn't deferred to ~6am (the mini deep-sleeps overnight; diagnosed via morning_diag 2026-07-06 — START=06:00 with daily_focus clean in 1 try, so NOT an AppStream issue). Uses sudo -n (needs passwordless sudo for pmset). Reversible: sudo pmset repeat cancel.

probe_readiness._note: MANUAL-ONLY (weekdays [] -> never auto-runs). `lucy rerun probe_readiness` runs the orchestrator's --probe-only mode on the mini (warm ownerville session) and prints per-source data-readiness for today's reports — read-only, runs NO reports. Built 2026-07-11 to validate the org_sales_board 'box_daily' readiness gate around Box's ~7-8am landing before cutting over from the old not_before=08:00 clock.

recruiting_backfill_juan._note: MANUAL-ONLY (weekdays [] -> never auto-runs). `lucy rerun recruiting_backfill_juan` backfills Juan Botero Berrio's blank recruiting weeks on Lucy 1 (needs the AppStream session). Added 2026-07-13 after moving him from sales_only -> confirmed (office 23703). One-off — safe to delete once his history is filled.

sunday_coverage._note: MANUAL-ONLY diagnostic. `lucy rerun sunday_coverage` pulls each ORG board CROSSTAB source on Lucy 1 and prints whether last Sunday's data is actually in the extract at pull time — diagnoses the 2026-07-13 Monday undercount (board missing the just-completed Sunday). Read-only.

install_leaders_call_agent._note: MANUAL: `lucy rerun install_leaders_call_agent` (re)installs the weekly Leader's Call launchd job (com.alphalete.leaders-call-mon, Mon 10:00am CST). The 7/10 reboot dropped it (the schedule-guard only reloads jobs that are ALREADY loaded), so it silently missed Mon 7/13 — reinstalling reloads it now AND makes it auto-load on future logins. weekdays [] = manual only.

morning_diag._note: MANUAL-ONLY diagnostic (weekdays [] -> never auto-runs). `lucy rerun morning_diag` prints, from the mini, when the 4am launchd orchestrator ACTUALLY started (from its per-run log filename: ~04:00 = mini awake, so the ~6am report delay is the AppStream session; ~06:00 = mini was asleep and launchd deferred the job) + pmset wake schedule/sleep timer + daily_focus attempt/fail count. Built 2026-07-06 to diagnose why AppStream/recruiting reports land ~6am not 4am.

alphalete_production._note: LIVE daily (Megan approved preview 2026-07-05). Combines Jolie's TWO manual morning screenshot posts into ONE dated thread in #alphalete-sales as Lucy: Daily Production, Team Sales (one per team from col CI), Highrollers of the day, + 3 rankings (Apps/New Internets/Wireless). Google-Sheet PNGs (Sales Board WE m.d) rendered off a hidden, auto-deleted COPY tab so the live sheet is never touched. Uses the tab CONTAINING yesterday -> Monday shows the fully-completed prior M-S week. source_type local = wave 1 (runs ~4am; yesterday's data is complete by then, before Jolie's ~6am). Preview instead of post: base_args ['--preview-dm','U045Z8N0ZQC','U04G5HJBGFN']. Replaces Jolie's 2 manual production posts — tell her to stop at cutover.

daily_metrics._sources_note: Internally orchestrates total_knocks, canceled_orders, ongoing_cancel, disconnects, churn, scheduled_6, order_log; gated on the dominant order_log source.

daily_focus._note: One pass over EVERY captainship tab. '--captainship all' reads CAPTAINSHIPS in daily_focus.py (Raf, Carlos, Sahil Multani, Chan Park, Jose Antonio Chavez). Add a captain there + a tab in the Sheet — no schedule_config edit needed. (Replaced the split daily_focus_raf/daily_focus_carlos entries 2026-06-26.)

weather_alert._note: Time-critical: posts to #alphalete-sales just before the 7am metrics thread.

captainship_activations._note: Per-captain sibling of fiber_activations: one Fiber dashboard pull -> 5 captain tabs (violet + shared country) + 6 PNGs to Downloads, Drive upload ON by default (non-fatal if disabled). NO Slack. Gated on the same fiber_pss source. Wednesday structure-only-inserts the new WE row.

org_sales_board._note: Fills the SANDBOX 'Copy of' tab daily, FILL-ONLY — the Tuesday auto-rollover was removed, so the weekly rollover is MANUAL (a person Monday night). Auto-compares vs the VA tab. Intentionally targets the sandbox; the real tab stays guarded (see _warn). TIMING (Megan 2026-07-11): runs in its ORDER (8), gated on DATA readiness — the not_before '08:00' clock was removed. The board's last-landing source is BOX (extract refreshes ~7-8am); data_sources now includes 'tableau:box_daily' with a box_daily readiness probe, so the orchestrator holds the board until Box has the latest completed day, then it runs (no more fixed 8am wait when Box is in early). The email (org_sales_board_email) depends_on this, so it follows right after. The 2:30pm com.alphalete.board-catchup remains the backstop. ONLY JE stays a day behind (inherent — the VAs' manual board is too). COMPARE DEFERRED (Megan 2026-07-14): the fill now runs with --skip-compare. Comparing right after the 4am fill pitted us against a VA tab the VAs hadn't touched yet, so every cell we were merely AHEAD on counted as a difference and the fill closed INCOMPLETE every morning. The real compare is report_id 'board_compare', which runs at 9am CST once the VAs have finished keying.
org_sales_board._warn: Scheduled on the SANDBOX 'Copy of' tab ONLY. The real VA tab stays guarded (--real refuses live runs + a Copy-of-only assert); a real-tab cutover needs that guard relaxed first — do NOT point this at the real tab unattended without that work.

org_sales_board_email._note: Emails the Org Sales Board as exact-sheet screenshots (copy tab, Sheets PDF-export render — no browser). depends_on org_sales_board so it only runs AFTER the fill is DONE (fresh numbers). PROVING (Megan 2026-07-04): base_args '' (no --preview) sends to the proving list — Maud + Rafael + Megan — off the COPY tab; at go-live swap to '--distro' (Alphalete Org Owners / Carlos' / Raf's Captain Team). source_type 'tableau' only to phase it right after the board — it has no Tableau pull (reads the sheet via OAuth).

cadence._note: Monday (0) EXCLUDED (Megan 2026-07-13): last week's Sunday lands in the sources only through Monday afternoon, so a morning email undercounts. On Mondays the board+email run in the afternoon via board_catchup.sh instead. Tue-Sun email in the morning batch as usual.

install_retail_catchup_agent._note: NOT a report — a one-shot LaunchAgent installer, run via `lucy rerun install_retail_catchup_agent` (same pattern as install_je_agent). on_scheduler:true ONLY so registry.load_config keeps it (rerun looks it up there); cadence.weekdays [] means the 4am orchestrator NEVER auto-runs it. Regens + launchctl-bootstraps com.alphalete.retail-catchup on the mini — a Monday 3pm CST re-pull of Retail NL + Retail Internet + JE, so the weekend's late-posting (day-behind) retail days land before the Monday-night manual rollover. source_type 'local' skips the tableau/appstream chrome guard. Not a Hub card. Re-runnable any time.

install_board_catchup_agent._note: NOT a report — a one-shot LaunchAgent installer, run via `lucy rerun install_board_catchup_agent` (same pattern as install_retail_catchup_agent). on_scheduler:true ONLY so registry.load_config keeps it (rerun looks it up there); cadence.weekdays [] means the 4am orchestrator NEVER auto-runs it. Regens + launchctl-bootstraps com.alphalete.board-catchup on the mini — a DAILY 2:30pm CST re-pull of the day-behind sections (Retail NL/Internet/JE + BOX + Frontier) for the current week, so yesterday's late-posting numbers land the same day and, on Monday, the just-closed Sunday lands before the Monday-night manual rollover. BOX is week-pinned so it re-pulls the right week even after its view rolls. source_type 'local' skips the tableau/appstream chrome guard. Not a Hub card. Re-runnable any time. TIME KNOB lives in the plist (confirm the real post time this Sunday and tune).

install_board_probe_agent._note: NOT a report — a one-shot LaunchAgent installer, run via `lucy rerun install_board_probe_agent` (same pattern as install_board_catchup_agent). Regens + launchctl-bootstraps com.alphalete.board-probe on the mini — a MONDAY-ONLY, READ-ONLY diagnostic that runs sunday_coverage at 7:15/7:45/8:15am CST to measure whether :refresh=yes (commit 378a275) delivers COMPLETE last-Sunday data early enough to email the board before 8am (Raf's deadline), and which week BOX returns (box=N = its pin is still broken). Writes NOTHING to any Sheet. Read results: `lucy logtail board-probe 'SUMMARY|box' 12`. REMOVE after the Monday timing is nailed (launchctl bootout gui/UID/com.alphalete.board-probe). on_scheduler:true ONLY so registry.load_config keeps it (rerun looks it up there); cadence.weekdays [] means the 4am orchestrator NEVER auto-runs it. source_type 'local' skips the tableau/appstream chrome guard.
install_board_probe_agent.display_name: Install: Monday board timing probe LaunchAgent

texas_de_brazil._note: Maud's Texas de Brazil monthly competition standings, as a remote-runnable handle. cadence.weekdays [] = the 4am orchestrator NEVER auto-runs it — its real schedule is the DAILY 7:45am CST launchd job (com.alphalete.texas-de-brazil-745, installed via install_texas_de_brazil_agent) on Lucy 1 (the mini). This handle exists so `lucy rerun texas_de_brazil --dry-run` (build the PDF + print the delivery plan, NO posts) and `lucy rerun texas_de_brazil --send` (LIVE: post the PDF AS Lucy to #alphalete-sales + #alphalete-lvl1-chat + iMessage the 'Alphalete A-Team Chat' group) both work. The report lives in the shared Report Library (Sheet); its module is the local cache the Hub materializes. Default (no args) is DRY-RUN — pass --send for a live post. Month is auto-derived. source_type 'local' (the module runs its own isolated headless Chrome for the PDF).

install_texas_de_brazil_agent._note: NOT a report — a one-shot LaunchAgent installer, run via `lucy rerun install_texas_de_brazil_agent` (same pattern as install_je_agent). on_scheduler:true ONLY so registry.load_config keeps it (rerun looks it up there); cadence.weekdays [] means the 4am orchestrator NEVER auto-runs it. Regens + launchctl-bootstraps com.alphalete.texas-de-brazil-745 on Lucy 1 (the mini) — Maud's daily 7:45am CST Texas de Brazil competition standings PDF, posted AS Lucy to #alphalete-sales + #alphalete-lvl1-chat + iMessaged to the 'Alphalete A-Team Chat' group. The wrapper materializes the latest library code, runs chrome_guard, then runs the report with --send. source_type 'local' skips the tableau/appstream chrome guard (the wrapper handles Chrome). Not a Hub card. Re-runnable any time.
install_texas_de_brazil_agent.display_name: Install: Texas de Brazil daily LaunchAgent

install_je_agent._note: NOT a report — a one-shot LaunchAgent installer, run via `lucy rerun install_je_agent`. on_scheduler:true ONLY so registry.load_config keeps it (rerun looks it up there); cadence.weekdays [] means the 4am orchestrator NEVER auto-runs it. It regens + launchctl-bootstraps com.alphalete.je-sunday-catchup on the mini. source_type 'local' skips the tableau/appstream chrome guard. Not a Hub card. Safe to leave here; re-runnable any time.

install_orchestrator_agent._note: NOT a report — re-bootstraps the day-orchestrator LaunchAgent itself, run via `lucy rerun install_orchestrator_agent`. FIXES the 2026-07-07 finding: the mini was awake at 4am (up 14d, no sleep) yet the batch started at 6am — launchd was honoring a STALE loaded schedule while the plist file already said Hour 4 (a git pull updates the file but never reloads launchd). This does launchctl bootout+bootstrap of com.alphalete.day-orchestrator so launchd picks up the 4am schedule. on_scheduler:true ONLY so registry keeps it; weekdays [] = never auto-runs. source_type 'local'. Safe/idempotent; the plist has no RunAtLoad so it won't trigger an immediate run. Re-run any time launchd drifts.

install_orchestrator_guard_agent._note: NOT a report — installs the SELF-HEAL for launchd schedule drift, run via `lucy rerun install_orchestrator_guard_agent` (on Lucy 2 too: add `--machine "Lucy 2"`). Bootstraps com.alphalete.orchestrator-schedule-guard, a launchd job that fires DAILY at 02:45 (before the earliest daily job, appstream-morning at 03:00) and runs schedule_guard.py, which re-bootstraps EVERY loaded, timed com.alphalete.* job from its current plist. WHY: `git pull` updates a plist FILE but never reloads launchd, so launchd kept firing stale boot-time schedules — the 4am orchestrator fired 6am for 15 days (2026-07-07/-08/-09, 4 recurrences) and brand-audit-noon once fired 2pm; every timed job carries the same risk. The guard reloads them all every night so launchd can NEVER hold a stale one and nobody has to remember to reinstall. Skips KeepAlive/always-on jobs and high-frequency pollers (rc-autoread, resume-pushing). on_scheduler:true ONLY so registry keeps it; weekdays [] = never auto-runs. source_type 'local'. Safe/idempotent; no committed plist has RunAtLoad so neither installing the guard nor its nightly reloads fire any job immediately. Run once per machine to install; re-run any time to refresh the guard itself.
install_orchestrator_guard_agent.display_name: Install: nightly guard that keeps EVERY timed report's schedule from drifting

schedule_audit._note: NOT a report — read-only audit of every timed launchd job on THIS machine, run via `lucy rerun schedule_audit` (Lucy 2: add `--machine "Lucy 2"`). Runs schedule_guard.py --audit, which lists each loaded, committed, timed com.alphalete.* job and the Hour:Minute (+ weekday) it's SCHEDULED to fire — so you can see at a glance what the schedule guard covers. Does NOT reload anything. For the actual-vs-scheduled START of the 4am batch specifically, use `lucy rerun morning_diag` (its DRIFT= field). on_scheduler:true ONLY so registry keeps it; weekdays [] = never auto-runs. source_type 'local'.
schedule_audit.display_name: Schedule audit — what each timed job is set to fire at

send_email_preview._note: NOT a report — renders + SENDS the current day's checkpoint email from today's saved day_state so you can VIEW the email format on demand (runs NO reports). Run via `lucy rerun send_email_preview` (add `--final` for the end-of-day summary, `--machine "Lucy 2"` for the other box). The Remaining-runs-later-today section reads this machine's real installed jobs, so it must run ON the mini to be accurate. on_scheduler:true ONLY so registry keeps it; weekdays [] = never auto-runs. source_type 'local'.
send_email_preview.display_name: Send a preview of the daily email

install_frontier_sunday_agent._note: NOT a report — installs the Frontier OPT Sunday 6pm job, run via `lucy rerun install_frontier_sunday_agent`. Bootstraps com.alphalete.frontier-sunday-6pm (Sunday 18:00), which waits for Credico's Sunday email then fills (frontier_sunday.py retries until the 2 sales PDFs are in the inbox). Frontier was moved OFF the Monday 4am batch (Megan 2026-07-09) — the completed week is in Sunday's ~1:30pm daily email, so 6pm has it. Covered by the nightly schedule guard. on_scheduler:true ONLY so registry keeps it; weekdays [] = never auto-runs. source_type 'local'.

board_compare._note: Full copy-vs-VA comparison of the Org Sales Board, run DAILY at 9am CST (not_before 09:00) — deliberately NOT straight after the 4am fill. At 4am the VAs have keyed nothing, so the automation is ahead on every cell it pulled and the compare is pure noise (it marked the fill INCOMPLETE every morning). By 9am the VAs are done, so a difference here is a REAL disagreement worth looking at. Read-only (sheet vs sheet, no Tableau). Writes every disagreeing cell to output/logs/org_sales_board_compare-*.log — read it with `lucy logtail org_sales_board_compare`. For the every-single-cell go-live gate (incl. rows past 1000) run `lucy rerun board_compare --all`.

resume_pushing._note: Carlos's ApplicantStream office-11580 resume extract + send-to-AI, as a remote-runnable handle. cadence.weekdays [] = the 4am orchestrator NEVER auto-runs it — its real schedule is the 8am launchd job (com.alphalete.resume-pushing, installed via install_resume_pushing_agent). This entry exists so `lucy rerun resume_pushing --dry-run` (safe probe) and `lucy rerun resume_pushing` (LIVE) work on Lucy 2, and so the Hub card's Dry Run / Run Live buttons have a path. SEND-TO-AI IS IRREVERSIBLE — always --dry-run first. source_type 'appstream' so rerun runs the chrome-guard first.

install_resume_pushing_agent._note: NOT a report — a one-shot LaunchAgent installer, run via `lucy rerun install_resume_pushing_agent` (same pattern as install_je_agent). on_scheduler:true ONLY so registry.load_config keeps it (rerun looks it up there); cadence.weekdays [] means the 4am orchestrator NEVER auto-runs it. Regens + launchctl-bootstraps com.alphalete.resume-pushing on Lucy 2 — Carlos's ApplicantStream office-11580 resume extract + send-to-AI, every 10 min 8am-10pm CST Sun+Mon-Fri (plist fires q10min; resume_pushing_10min.sh gates the window + skips Sat). Runs on the machine's own AppStream session (Lucy 2 = Carlos's account). NOTE: the scheduled run is LIVE (send-to-AI is IRREVERSIBLE) — dry-run first with `bash deploy/resume_pushing_10min.sh --dry-run` before installing. Re-runnable any time.

carlos_captainship_headcount._note: Carlos Captainship Headcount — Maud's weekly Monday fill of the 'Captainship Head count' tab in *All In One - CARLOS* (each active owner's Rep Count from Tableau ATTTRACKER-B2B / D2D1-PAGERV3, retotaled + sorted high->low). cadence.weekdays [] = the 4am orchestrator NEVER auto-runs it — its real schedule is the Monday 7am launchd job (com.alphalete.carlos-captainship-headcount-mon, installed via install_carlos_captainship_headcount_agent) on Lucy 2. This handle exists so `lucy rerun carlos_captainship_headcount --dry-run` (safe probe) and `lucy rerun carlos_captainship_headcount` (LIVE fill) work, and matches the Hub card's Dry Run / Run Live buttons. source_type 'tableau' so rerun runs the chrome-guard + readiness gate. Runs off Lucy 2's warm Tableau session.

Runs off Lucy 2's warm Tableau session.

uninstall_fls_agent._note: NOT a report — one-shot LaunchAgent REMOVER for the retired First/Last Sale report (Megan 2026-07-06). Run via `lucy rerun uninstall_fls_agent`. on_scheduler:true ONLY so registry.load_config keeps it (rerun looks it up there); cadence.weekdays [] means the 4am orchestrator NEVER auto-runs it. launchctl-boots-out + deletes com.alphalete.first-last-sale-mon on the mini so the Mon 2pm job stops with zero mini visit. Idempotent — safe to re-run. Once confirmed gone, this entry + uninstall_agent.py can be dropped.

install_social_scanner_agent._note: NOT a report — one-shot LaunchAgent installer, run via `lucy rerun install_social_scanner_agent` (same pattern as install_je_agent / install_fls_agent). on_scheduler:true ONLY so registry.load_config keeps it (rerun looks it up there); cadence.weekdays [] means the 4am orchestrator NEVER auto-runs it. install_brand_audit_agent._note: NOT a report — one-shot LaunchAgent installer, run via `lucy rerun install_brand_audit_agent` (same pattern as install_social_scanner_agent). on_scheduler:true ONLY so registry.load_config keeps it (rerun looks it up there); cadence.weekdays [] means the 4am orchestrator NEVER auto-runs it. Re-bootstraps com.alphalete.brand-audit-noon from the committed Hour-12 plist. FIXES the 2026-07-07 drift: the mini's loaded launchd schedule was firing this at 14:00 (2pm) though deploy/com.alphalete.brand-audit-noon.plist has said Hour 12 since it was created — same stale-loaded-schedule class as install_orchestrator_agent. bootout+bootstrap makes launchd pick up noon. Re-runnable any time launchd drifts.

daily_rep_breakdown._timeout_note: Heavy: ~30-owner ownerville scrape (60m budget) + Tableau fill (35m). The flat 45m orchestrator cap killed it mid-scrape every run, which corrupted the sheet. Runs last (P3) so a long run blocks nothing.
daily_rep_breakdown._freshness_note: Megan's example: least important day-to-day, but should be as current as possible by Monday morning.
daily_rep_breakdown._depends_note: Runs AFTER org_sales_board via SOFT `after` ordering (Megan 2026-07-13): this is the heaviest run (~90m) and must never start before the board (or the board gets stuck behind it for the browser). But a board GLITCH must never skip this — so `after` (unblocks once the board hits ANY terminal state: done/incomplete/FAILED/missed), NOT depends_on (which would strand it on a board failure).

brand_audit._disabled_reason: Not a priority — excluded from the orchestrator so its noon slot doesn't hold the morning final email. Runs on its own standalone noon launchd job (com.alphalete.brand-audit-noon); listed in the summary email as REMAINING via runs_at.

att_focus_raf._note: AppStream + Tableau OPT; gated on the Tableau OPT source. run.py defaults to the current week.

alphalete_org_focus._todo: confirm opt_all picks the current week with no --week (verify in the dry-run week).

alphalete_org_retail._note: Re-run ONLY the Retail OPT step of the Alphalete Org 1on1s report, via `lucy rerun alphalete_org_retail` — so a single-step failure (e.g. a flaky SARA Plus Tableau scrape) refills without re-doing the other 5 steps. on_scheduler:true ONLY so registry.load_config keeps it (rerun looks it up there); cadence.weekdays [] means the 4am orchestrator NEVER auto-runs it (the full run via alphalete_org_focus already includes Retail). source_type 'tableau' so the Chrome-collision guard runs first. Not a Hub card.

leaders_call._off_scheduler_note: Run by the dedicated com.alphalete.leaders-call-mon launchd job at Mon 10:00am CST (deploy/leaders_call_mon.sh), NOT the morning orchestrator, so it fires at a fixed 10am. Maud 2026-06-29.

idempotency._note: inserts a new week column; existing guard overwrites WE column if already at B (verified 2026-06-23).

idempotency._warn: inserts a new week column — confirm it won't double-insert on retry before cutover.

frontier_opt._note: Auto-ingest via --email: pulls the 3 Frontier Events PDFs from reports@credicousa.com (frontier_email_source) into a temp dir, then fills. Manual Upload card kept as fallback. Sales PDFs land DAILY ~1:30pm CT (completed week is in Sunday's daily, so the Monday 4am run has it); Quality Scorecard lags ~2wk by design. readiness._probe_email gates on the daily PDFs. Verify not wired yet (opt_frontier has no manifest).

rashad_metrics._disabled_reason: BUILDING 2026-06-28. 4 of 7 metrics wired (order_log, canceled_orders, disconnects, sales_6plus) via the org-wide-pull → filter-to-Rashad pattern. Posts AS Lucy by reusing the SAME Slack token path as daily_metrics (no separate token — on the mini that file is Lucy). Remaining 3 are FLAGGED, each needs a Tableau asset that doesn't exist: ongoing_cancel (a Rashad/org-wide cancel-rates view — current RafExpanded is Raf-only computed), churn (a Rashad churn view — INTLocalOffice bakes owner=Raf; also sheet-coupled, needs a Rashad tab or direct-render), knocks_gaps (Rashad's rep roster — Disposition-by-Rep has no owner column). Flip true only after a clean dry-run of the 4 wired on the mini + Megan's go-ahead (and the 3 flagged either built or accepted as omitted).
rashad_metrics._sources_note: Single-office cut of the daily metrics for owner 'Rashad Reed', posted to his PRIVATE #elevate-sales (C0B3KTCCMT7) as the Lucy reporting bot. Internally orchestrates order_log + canceled_orders + disconnects today (more as wired); gated on the dominant order_log source.
rashad_metrics._base_args_note: LIVE: pulls all metric slugs for Rashad (order_log+rep_activations, sales_6plus, cancels, ongoing_cancel, disconnects, churn, knocks_gaps) + POSTS to #elevate-sales. Flipped to DAILY (every day, not-before 06:00) 2026-06-30 after a verified 10/10 thread; churn grid-range + order_log owner-gate + sidebar-nav stale-session fixes all landed same day.

aya_metrics._sources_note: Single-office cut of the daily metrics for owner 'Aya Al-Khafaji', posted to her PRIVATE #indelible-sales (C0AA85Y3FPE) as the Lucy reporting bot. Same machinery as rashad_metrics (reuses its _metric_cmd/_run_one) with Aya's Tableau views: INTAYA/WirelessAYA churn, AyaExpanded cancel-rates, AyaINTABP. order_log/sales_6plus/cancels/disconnects auto-scope via --owner; knocks impersonates her OV office (KNOCKS_OFFICE). Built 2026-07-10.
aya_metrics._base_args_note: LIVE: pulls all metric slugs for Aya + POSTS to #indelible-sales. Continue-on-failure. RECOMMEND a `lucy rerun aya_metrics --dry-run` on the mini to verify her NEW views (INTAYA/WirelessAYA/AyaExpanded) before trusting the first live run; ABP + the --owner metrics reuse proven patterns.
aya_metrics._sequence_note: order 7.5 → runs as the NEXT report right after Rashad (order 7) in the list, NOT via depends_on (Megan 2026-07-10: don't gate Aya on Rashad succeeding — just sequence her next). No independent 6am start; run_order sorts by 'order' so 7.5 slots between Rashad (7) and the next numbered report (8). Readiness still gates the Tableau pulls.

rashad_churn._note: weekdays=[] → NEVER auto-runs on schedule (scheduled_today filters by weekday); it exists ONLY so `lucy rerun rashad_churn` can run Rashad's churn fill on-demand on the mini. Fills the 'Metrics Reports - Rashad Reed' churn tabs (today's snapshot, sheet-only — churn_pull defaults to --skip-slack). Flip weekdays to daily + wire the #elevate-sales post once verified.

rashad_orderlog._note: MANUAL re-post — `lucy rerun rashad_orderlog` posts JUST Rashad's Order Log + Rep Activations LIVE to #elevate-sales (rashad_metrics.run --live --only order_log, which sets METRICS_CHANNEL_ID + posts AS Lucy into today's thread). weekdays=[] never auto-runs; the full rashad_metrics card owns the daily 10-metric post. Repurposed 2026-07-01 from the dry diagnostic once the non-default-owner order_log ALLREPS pull was fixed (commit 70874c1).

rashad_metrics_review._note: REVIEW run: `lucy rerun rashad_metrics_review` runs the full report --live but DMs it to Megan (U045Z8N0ZQC) via --channel, for review BEFORE going public to #elevate-sales. weekdays=[] never auto-runs.

rashad_churn_post._note: Churn-only LIVE post: `lucy rerun rashad_churn_post` -> rashad_metrics.run --only churn --live -> posts New Internet + Wireless Churn into TODAY's existing #elevate-sales Metrics thread (find_metrics_thread_ts locates today's header + replies in-thread, NOT a new top-level msg). weekdays=[] never auto-runs. Added 2026-06-30 to recover churn after the full run's churn post failed (other 8 already posted).

financial_report._note: Auto-ingest via --email: pulls this week's FINANCIAL SUMMARY .xlsx from ALL senders (financial_report.email_source: hubtruth/Amber, melissab/Coel, jsanchez/German) into a temp dir, then fills every matching ICD tab. Manual Upload card kept as fallback. Arrivals (CST): hubtruth Tue ~2:30pm, melissab Wed ~11am, jsanchez Mon/Tue -> all in by Wed midday, so a Thursday 4am run has the full week with a day of buffer. readiness._probe_email gates on this week's workbooks landing. Incremental: unmatched ICDs are never wiped (missing = not-yet-arrived). Verify not wired (no manifest yet).

tableau_screenshots._note: LIVE daily Tableau COUNTRY trackers (Download->Image, per-tracker page-1 crop_to_bar + bottom trim). Posts the 7 boards whose data IS current at 4:31 -- B2B Box is `late: True` in pages.py and rides the tableau_screenshots_box catch-up at ~7am instead, because its extract doesn't refresh until then (see that report's note). The header still lists Box in its normal slot, flagged as still coming. ONE run captures the boards once and posts them as Lucy into a dated thread 'Tableau Country Trackers M/D/YYYY' in EVERY channel (--orgs all): #alphalete-sales + #top-leaders-alphalete-org, #elevate-sales, #indelible-sales, #palace-sales, #elite-prime-sales. The boards are country-wide, so every channel gets identical images off the single capture -- one Tableau login a day. ADD A CHANNEL = one line in slack_post.ORG_CHANNELS, nothing else. A channel that fails does NOT sink the others: it lands in the run manifest's failed list, so the Hub card shows a per-channel checklist + a 'Retry failed only' button that re-posts just the misses (--orgs <them> --replace). source_type tableau = wave 2. Went live 2026-07-05. To preview instead of post: base_args ['--preview-dm','U04G5HJBGFN'].

tableau_screenshots_box._note: The ~7am B2B BOX catch-up for the Tableau Country Trackers. Box's extract refreshes ~7-8am with the prior day's FINAL numbers, so at 4:31 it posted yesterday's figures into every channel every morning (Carlos flagged it 2026-07-16, 'Box isn't updated, it was off yesterday too'). The other 7 boards are correct at 4:31, so they still post then -- pages.py marks Box `late: True`, the morning run drops it, and THIS run posts it with --late-only once its data is actually in. NOT A CLOCK: data_sources 'tableau:box_daily' is the SAME readiness probe org_sales_board already waits on, and ReadinessCache is per-run + shared, so this costs no extra Tableau pull and Box posts the moment its extract lands (typically well before 7) -- never later than the probe's 08:00 fail-open floor, which exists so the gate can never skip the post. Appends Box's image into today's EXISTING thread in every channel (find-or-create by dated title, same routing as the morning run) and clears the header's 'data lands ~7am' note. Idempotent per tracker: a re-run leaves a channel that already has today's Box alone. Own report_id/manifest/status file so it can't overwrite the morning card's per-channel checklist.

tableau_preview._note: MANUAL-ONLY preview harness (weekdays [] -> never auto-runs; reachable via lucy rerun). Captures the tracker(s) and DMs them to Megan via --preview-dm, posting NOTHING to the channels — safe for dialing in a crop. (Was briefly set live to re-post the corrected nds/b2b crops 2026-07-05; reverted to DM-only.)

churn_eveliz_fix._note: MANUAL-ONLY (weekdays [] -> never auto-runs; reachable via lucy rerun). Targeted re-run of ONLY the Eveliz Wright (B2B) churn tab to apply the Eveliz Roca->Eveliz Wright alias after her Tableau rename (team flagged 2026-07-05). Remove after use.

opt_phase._note: MANUAL-ONLY (weekdays [] -> never auto-runs; the weekly Focus run already includes OPT). Lets you re-run JUST the OPT phase when the Focus completion email notes some ICDs missing their OPT section, instead of the whole ~30-min AppStream+OPT run: lucy rerun opt_phase  (or  lucy rerun opt_phase --only "Marcellus Butler" for one ICD).

kick_poller._note: MANUAL-ONLY (weekdays [] -> never auto-runs). Bootstrap: lucy rerun kick_poller restarts the mini-control poller so it reloads its code — works even when the running poller predates restart_poller/self-reload, because rerun spawns a fresh subprocess. After the poller has self-reload, lucy update alone suffices; keep this for a forced restart.
